rationale: Atom directly covers U.S. healthcare delivery system: providers, settings, payers, and regulators

Atom covers education, licensure, and certification ladders by role

Atom directly teaches word roots, prefixes, and suffixes for medical communication
Atom's decoding strategy explicitly targets interpreting unfamiliar terms from word parts
Atom covers common abbreviations and explicitly includes the Joint Commission do-not-use list
Atom reinforces medical-communication vocabulary
Atom addresses documentation standards through abbreviation use
Atom directly covers levels of organization, planes, cavities, and directional terms
Atom identifies the 11 major body systems and primary functions with representative disorders
Atom directly teaches the four ethical principles and applies them to dilemmas
Atom covers HIPAA Privacy, Security, and Breach rules plus patient confidentiality in practice
Atom covers Patient Bill of Rights and the five elements of informed consent
Atom directly addresses scope, licensure, delegation, and practice-act variation
Atom covers standard precautions and the six-link chain of infection with transmission modes
Atom teaches soap-and-water and ABHR technique plus WHO Five Moments
Atom covers PPE selection and correct donning/doffing sequence with skill check
Atom teaches body mechanics, safe transfers, and lift-equipment decision-making
Atom covers RACE, PASS, emergency codes, and evacuation priorities
Atom directly addresses verbal and nonverbal communication with patients and teams
Atom covers communication barriers and specific adaptations

Atom reinforces communication adaptations for diverse patient populations
